title: betula ermanii var. japonica
intro: Betula ermanii var. japonica, also known as Japanese paper birch, is a deciduous tree species native to East Asia, characterized by its white bark and slender branches. The medicinal properties of Betula ermanii var. japonica include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which are attributed to its high content of betulin and betulone. This plant is known for its various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and acting as an expectorant to ease coughs. The herbal preparations of Betula ermanii var. japonica include infusions, tinctures, and decoctions, which are often used to treat colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
